merged_events.event_description: Diplomatic relations and the U.S. Legation in Havana were established when U.S. Envoy Extraordinary and Minister Plenipotentiary Herbert Goldsmith Squiers presented his credentials to the Government of the Republic of Cuba.

evidence.quote: Diplomatic relations and the U.S. Legation in Havana were established on May 27, 1902, when U.S. Envoy Extraordinary and Minister Plenipotentiary Herbert Goldsmith Squiers presented his credentials to the Government of the Republic of Cuba.

merged_events.event_description: The U.S. Legation in Havana was elevated to Embassy status when General Enoch H. Crowder was appointed Ambassador.

evidence.quote: Following an act of Congress, the U.S. Legation in Havana, Cuba, was raised to Embassy status on February 10, 1923, when General Enoch H. Crowder was appointed Ambassador.

merged_events.event_description: The United States severed diplomatic relations with Cuba, citing crippling limitations placed on the U.S. Mission by the Cuban government.

evidence.quote: The United States severed diplomatic relations with Cuba on January 3, 1961, citing unwarranted action by the Government of Cuba that placed crippling limitations on the ability of the United States Mission to carry on its normal diplomatic and consular functions.

merged_events.event_description: A U.S. Interests Section was established in Havana under the protection of the Swiss Government.

evidence.quote: A U.S. Interests Section was established in Havana under the protection of the Swiss Government on September 1, 1977.

merged_events.event_description: The United States and Cuba resumed diplomatic relations and elevated their respective Interests Sections to Embassy status.

evidence.quote: The United States and Cuba resumed diplomatic relations on July 20, 2015, when both countries elevated their respective Interests Sections to Embassy status.

merged_events.event_description: Enoch Herbert Crowder presented credentials as the first Ambassador Extraordinary and Plenipotentiary to Cuba, elevating the mission from a legation to an embassy.

evidence.quote: Enoch Herbert Crowder, Ambassador Extraordinary and Plenipotentiary. Appointed: 1923-02-10. Credentials presented: 1923-03-05. Left post on 1927-05-28.

matched.notes: Exact match on date (1902-05-27) and status (Legation). RDCR confirms establishment of diplomatic relations and U.S. Legation.

CSV date 1923-02-10 matches RDCR (Event 1). POCOM (Event 5) gives 1923-03-05 as credential presentation date, but RDCR provides the explicit institutional elevation date of 1923-02-10, which takes priority. No discrepancy.
Exact match on date (1961-01-03) and status (None). RDCR confirms severance of diplomatic relations.
Exact match on date (1977-09-01) and status (Interests). RDCR confirms establishment of U.S. Interests Section in Havana.
Exact match on date (2015-07-20) and status (Embassy). RDCR confirms resumption of diplomatic relations and elevation to Embassy status.
